step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture connects video content originators and media outlets. Originators upload files through ELB (secured by WAF) to EC2 App instances, then directly to S3. Video jobs are managed asynchronously using SQS, which schedules EC2 Batch and EC2 Transcoding/QC servers. Media outlets fetch files directly from S3 or via CloudFront for CDN distribution.

graph.name: Extreme Reach Adbridge Platform Architecture
graph.notes: Handles 80% of US ad traffic. Uses SQS to queue and prioritize time-sensitive media transcoding and quality control jobs.

nodes.name: Content Originators
nodes.notes: Advertisers and brands uploading video content.

nodes.name: ELB
nodes.notes: Balances ingress API, UI, and Java agent traffic.

nodes.name: WAF Firewall
nodes.notes: Filters and monitors web traffic for security threat blocking.

nodes.name: EC2 APP
nodes.notes: Hosts main UI, API services, and upload manager.

nodes.name: S3 Ad Cloud
nodes.notes: Stores master files and converted broadcast-ready versions.

nodes.name: SQS Asynch
nodes.notes: Prioritized job queues for asynchronous transcoding and QC workflows.

nodes.name: EC2 BATCH
nodes.notes: Executes custom back-end batch tasks and orchestration.

nodes.name: EC2 QC TRANS
nodes.notes: Runs quality control and video transcoding processes.

nodes.name: CloudFront
nodes.notes: Caches and distributes digital video advertising.

edges.notes: Sends API and UI upload requests
Routes validated traffic to App Tier
Direct file upload via Java app/UI
Reads masters and saves transcoded versions
S3 origins digital assets to CloudFront
Delivers digital video ads to outlets
Direct download of TV ads from closest S3 region
